title: Heaps / Priority Queues
description: Go's container/heap package requires implementing an interface. Master the IntHeap pattern, Push/Pop, and min-heap usage — these power top-K, merge-K-sorted, and Dijkstra problems.

sections.title: Implementing the heap interface
sections.explanation: Go's heap package requires your type to implement **sort.Interface** (Len, Less, Swap) plus **Push** and **Pop**. Define an IntHeap type as `[]int`, implement the five methods, and use `heap.Init()`, `heap.Push()`, `heap.Pop()`. Less determines min vs max: `<` for min-heap, `>` for max-heap.

exercise.prompt: Complete the IntHeap min-heap implementation. Init a heap with [5, 3, 8, 1], pop the minimum twice, and print each popped value.

validation.message: Use < in Less for min-heap, heap.Init to heapify, heap.Pop to extract min

exercise.hint: h[i] < h[j] makes it a min-heap. heap.Init(h) heapifies. heap.Pop(h) returns the smallest.

sections.title: Max-heap variant
sections.explanation: To make a max-heap, simply reverse the Less comparison to **h[i] > h[j]**. Everything else stays the same. This is useful for problems like finding the K largest elements, where you maintain a max-heap and pop the maximum.

exercise.prompt: Create a MaxHeap, init with [10, 40, 20, 30], pop the top two elements (largest first), and print them.
exercise.hint: h[i] > h[j] makes it a max-heap. heap.Init(h) heapifies. Pop returns 40 then 30.

sections.title: Top-K pattern
sections.explanation: To find the K smallest elements, maintain a **max-heap of size K**: push each element, and when the heap exceeds K, pop the max. What remains are the K smallest. For K largest, use a min-heap of size K. This is O(n log k) — better than sorting O(n log n) when k is small.

exercise.prompt: Find the 3 smallest elements in [8, 2, 7, 1, 5, 3, 9] using a max-heap of size 3. Sort and print the result.

validation.message: Maintain heap size at 3 by popping when it exceeds K

exercise.hint: Push each element. When heap size exceeds 3, pop the max. The 3 remaining are the smallest.
